verses.text: Four times David cries 'how long?' — feeling forgotten, his face hidden from God, sorrow daily in his heart. The delay of help tests his faith. Even believers may feel God has forgotten, and may pour out that ache to Him.

Four times David cries 'how long?' — feeling forgotten, his face hidden from God, sorrow daily in his heart. The delay of help tests his faith. Even believers may feel God has forgotten, and may pour out that ache to Him.
He begs God to consider and answer, lest he sleep the sleep of death and his foes rejoice. He pleads his very life. Honest prayer lays our fears and our enemies plainly before God.
He begs God to consider and answer, lest he sleep the sleep of death and his foes rejoice. He pleads his very life. Honest prayer lays our fears and our enemies plainly before God.

Yet he turns: 'I have trusted in thy mercy; my heart shall rejoice,' and he resolves to sing to the LORD. Faith outlasts the questioning. The same psalm that began in 'how long?' ends in song, for trust finds its way back to praise.

Yet he turns: 'I have trusted in thy mercy; my heart shall rejoice,' and he resolves to sing to the LORD. Faith outlasts the questioning. The same psalm that began in 'how long?' ends in song, for trust finds its way back to praise.
